title: Managed audit manual sandbox connection credential resolver signed human approval artifact contract intake

signedArtifactContract.purpose: Define the non-secret signed human approval artifact shape required before any later managed audit credential resolver runtime shell discussion.

requiredFields.label: Artifact id
requiredFields.purpose: Trace the signed approval artifact across Node, Java, and mini-kv evidence.

requiredFields.label: Approval correlation id
requiredFields.purpose: Bind the signed artifact to the approval review chain.

requiredFields.label: Operator identity handle
requiredFields.acceptedShape: operator identity handle, no credential value
requiredFields.purpose: Identify the requesting operator without embedding secret material.

requiredFields.label: Signer identity handle
requiredFields.acceptedShape: signer identity handle, no private key
requiredFields.purpose: Identify the human signer without carrying signing keys or credentials.

requiredFields.label: Policy version
requiredFields.purpose: Bind the artifact to a known approval policy contract.

requiredFields.label: Artifact digest
requiredFields.acceptedShape: sha256 digest or equivalent stable digest
requiredFields.purpose: Prove artifact immutability without embedding raw secret payloads.

requiredFields.label: Issued at
requiredFields.purpose: Declare when the human approval artifact was issued.

requiredFields.label: Expires at
requiredFields.purpose: Prevent stale approval artifacts from being treated as current.

requiredFields.label: Review status
requiredFields.purpose: Keep this version contract-only and status-based.

requiredFields.label: No-network assertion
requiredFields.purpose: Assert this contract path sends no HTTP/TCP request.

requiredFields.label: Rollback/abort reference
requiredFields.purpose: Reference manual abort and rollback semantics without executing them.

prohibitedFields.reason: Credential values must not enter the signed approval artifact.
The contract may reference endpoint handles, not raw endpoint URLs.
A signed artifact may reference a signer handle, not a private key.
Provider configuration would turn this contract into implementation.
Resolver client configuration is not allowed in a contract-only intake.
No HTTP/TCP payload may be prepared or sent by v314.
Approval ledger writes remain outside this Node contract.
Schema migration SQL is prohibited in this intake.

rejectionReasons.message: The signed approval artifact contract fields are missing.
Credential values are not allowed; only handles and review statuses are allowed.
Raw endpoint URLs are not allowed; only endpoint handles may appear later.
Runtime shell implementation or invocation details are prohibited.
Ledger writes, schema migration, deployment, and rollback execution are prohibited.

noGoBoundaries.message: v314 must not implement a runtime shell.
v314 must not invoke a runtime shell.
v314 must not read managed audit credential values.
v314 must not parse or render raw endpoint URLs.
v314 must not instantiate secret providers or resolver clients.
v314 must not send HTTP/TCP requests.
v314 must not write approval ledger or schema state.
v314 must not automatically start Java, mini-kv, or external audit services.

upstreamEchoRequests.requestedEcho: Read-only echo of the Node v314 signed human approval artifact contract.
Non-participation receipt proving mini-kv does not store, validate, or become authority for signed approval artifacts.

necessityProof.blockerResolved: v312 paused the governance chain with five remaining prerequisites; v314 names the first concrete missing prerequisite and defines its non-secret contract shape.
necessityProof.consumer: Java v145 + mini-kv v138, then Node v315
necessityProof.whyV312CannotBeReused: v312 is a closure decision only; it lists signed-human-approval-artifact as missing but does not define the signed artifact contract fields that upstreams can echo.
necessityProof.existingReportReuseDecision: Reuse v312 as source state and v313 as the prerequisite catalog; create v314 only for the signed-human-approval-artifact contract intake.
necessityProof.stopCondition: Stop if the contract requires credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider/client configuration, HTTP/TCP, runtime shell implementation or invocation, ledger/schema writes, mini-kv authority, or automatic upstream start.

warnings.message: v314 defines a signed-human-approval-artifact contract only; credential handle approval, endpoint allowlist, no-network fixture, and abort/rollback semantics remain separate prerequisites.

recommendations.message: After v314 is archived, Java v145 and mini-kv v138 can run in parallel as read-only echo and non-participation receipt work.
Keep the signed approval artifact as handles, status, digest, and timestamps only; never add credential values, raw endpoint URLs, private keys, provider/client config, or HTTP/TCP payloads.

nextActions: Archive Node v314 as the contract-only signed human approval artifact intake.
After v314 is complete, Java v145 and mini-kv v138 can run in parallel as read-only echo and non-participation receipt work.
Node v315 must wait for both Java v145 and mini-kv v138 before verifying upstream echo alignment.
Stop immediately if the signed artifact contract tries to include credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider/client config, HTTP/TCP payloads, ledger/schema writes, or runtime shell behavior.
